items.title: XRP, HYPE funds are the bright spots as investors flee bitcoin, ether ETFs
items.description: Your day-ahead look for July 1, 2026

items.title: Bitcoin options traders load up on $50,000 puts and gold futures flash a death cross
items.description: Bitcoin options flows and a record gold OI suggest traders are bracing for further downside rather than a sustained recovery.

items.title: Aave logs biggest network-growth day in nearly 5 years as DeFi interest returns
items.description: The lending protocol added 1,806 new wallets in a single day, the most since October 2021, according to Santiment. The AAVE token has risen about 20% in a week even as the broader market slides.

items.title: Bitcoin’s 20% June crash looks even deadlier on the charts. Here’s why
items.description: Bitcoin dropped by 20% in June, but the monthly chart reveals something far more concerning.

items.title: Live markets: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs had their worst month ever in June, shedding $4.5 billion
items.description: The record outflow beat the previous worst month by 29% and came on nine consecutive days of redemptions to close the period.

items.title: Anthropic restores AI models Fable, Mythos after the U.S. lifts export controls
items.description: The government cleared Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 30, weeks after a cybersecurity finding triggered an export order that froze access for everyone.

items.title: Why Poland is the only EU country where crypto firms can't get a MiCA license
items.description: President Karol Nawrocki refuses to sign a law that gives the regulator power to approve companies, forcing tech founders to look outside their own borders for permission to operate.

items.title: XRP holds above $1 after leverage flush as network activity improves
items.description: Open interest has collapsed from last year’s highs while active addresses and ETF inflows rise, but XRP still needs to reclaim $1.10 before the chart turns cleaner.

items.title: Crypto enters Q3 with thinner liquidity but less leverage after Q2 reset: Talos
items.description: Bitcoin and Ether open interest fell sharply after $8.35 billion in long liquidations, while ETF outflows, weaker Strategy purchases and declining market depth reduced liquidity.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs lose record $4.5B in June, eclipsing Strategy's $1.25B raise
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs logged a record $4.5 billion in June outflows, pushing year-to-date totals to $5.5 billion, signaling an unprecedented pace of withdrawals.

items.title: Former Goliath Ventures CEO pleads guilty in $400M crypto Ponzi case
items.description: Former Goliath Ventures CEO Christopher Delgado pleaded guilty to fraud and money laundering and agreed to forfeit properties, vehicles, luxury goods and crypto wallets.

items.title: Taiwan’s legislature passes crypto, stablecoin regulations
items.description: Taiwan’s legislative branch has passed the nation’s first crypto and stablecoin rules, which aim to integrate the country with the global crypto market.

items.title: Anthropic to bring back Fable 5 as US lifts export controls
items.description: Claude Fable 5 will be redeployed with a new set of classifiers designed to identify and block a broader range of cybersecurity-related tasks.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Trump earned more from crypto than real estate in 2025, filings show
items.description: Memecoin sales and royalties made the most money for Trump last year, more than all of his golf courses combined.

items.title: Director of ‘47 Ronin’ who gambled Netflix funds on crypto gets 30 months
items.description: Carl Rinsch will serve two and a half years in prison for taking money meant for a Netflix show to buy Dogecoin, stocks and luxury goods.

items.title: Tech leads first half stock gains — but the biggest winners weren't in the U.S.
items.description: U.S. Big Tech stocks enjoyed strong gains — despite a sharp sell-off at the end of June — but were largely outperformed by their international counterparts.

items.title: Oil prices wobble as breakdown in U.S.-Iran talks casts doubt over peace process
items.description: Oil prices were lower on Wednesday after Iran said it would not meet with U.S. delegates for talks in Qatar, amplifying concern about the peace process.

items.title: Bulls are betting big on this global ETF that's deep in a bear market
items.description: U.S. stocks just keep winning, with the Nasdaq closing out its best quarter since 2020. Across the globe in China, things couldn't be more different.

items.title: Demand for riskier mortgages drops, as their advantages shrink
items.description: The spread between the 30-year fixed rate mortgage and adjustable rate loans is narrowing, causing demand for ARMs to weaken.

items.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
items.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

items.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
items.description: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

items.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

items.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
items.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: Five things we learnt from Trump’s financial disclosure
items.description: The US president earned more than $1bn from digital currency interests as well as real estate and stock trades

items.title: Uniqlo’s plan to dominate global fashion
items.description: The clothing retailer forged in Japan’s post-bubble economy is pinning its hopes on Europe and North America

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=II3_Rum8J5M! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Tesla deliveries are set to rise — no thanks to the U.S.
items.description: Europe should be a source of strength when Tesla posts its second-quarter delivery numbers later this week.

items.title: How to stay healthy in a heat wave — and medicines that put you at risk for heat exhaustion
items.description: Heatstroke can affect anyone, but people who take certain medications or have certain health conditions are at higher risk.

items.title: Nike earnings crushed Wall Street’s estimates — but here’s the catch
items.description: Nike’s profit and gross margins were buoyed by a tariff refund.

items.title: Why it’s too early to call a top on semiconductor stocks, according to these highly regarded analysts
items.description: Nomura analysts offer a deep dive into the semiconductor industry — and some bottlenecks that the market hasn’t even woken up to.

items.title: Student-loan borrowers face major changes that start today. Your repayment plan may be phased out.
items.description: SAVE plan notices are going out in July, and other repayment plans are being phased out or launched.

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.description: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”

items.title: Is the stock market open on Friday, or will it close early for the Fourth of July?
items.description: Since Independence Day falls on Saturday this year, here’s what to know about trading hours and mail delivery

items.title: Anthropic gets all-clear to let foreigners use latest model ahead of crucial IPO

items.title: Eurozone inflation falls more than expected to 2.8% in June
items.description: Price growth remains above central bank’s target for fourth straight month

items.title: How China’s green tech could boost its global finance ambitions
items.description: Clean tech leadership will help Beijing’s financial agenda, says former central bank chief economist

items.title: UK competition watchdog to widen probe into childcare market
items.description: CMA will now also review how different providers affect options and cost as well as the availability of places

items.title: Pancakes and holograms fail to draw crowds to Trump’s ‘Freedom 250’ fair
items.description: Great American State Fair highlights US political division on Washington’s National Mall

items.title: BlueCrest warns UK is bad for business after losing £200mn tax battle
items.description: Investment firm says country ‘no longer a serious contender’

items.title: Trump’s contradictions mangle a complex history of US trade
items.description: The president’s obsession with tariffs reflects a misreading of the past 250 years

items.title: June inflation in the Eurozone is good news for the ECB
items.description: Weaker headline and core inflation provide early indication that the energy shock is temporary

items.title: FirstFT: US stock markets record best quarter since 2020
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Democratic socialist wins Colorado primary and Trump administration green-lights latest Anthropic AI model

items.title: As Companies Race for Cheaper A.I. Options, This Start-Up Pitches a Solution
items.description: Together AI, which specializes in open-source artificial intelligence models, is now worth more than $8 billion.

items.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement Talks
items.description: July 1 could be the beginning of the end for a trade pact President Trump has long criticized — or the start of a path to its renewal.

items.title: The South Korean Mine at the Center of America’s Tungsten Push
items.description: Deep beneath a South Korean mountain, workers are reviving a mine that could challenge China’s grip on a metal vital to weapons, chips and industry.

items.title: Judges Strike Down Trump Rule on Loan Forgiveness for Public Servants
items.description: Two federal courts have blocked a new Trump administration rule that could have narrowed eligibility for a student loan forgiveness program for public servants.

items.title: During Traffic Stop, N.F.L. Reporter Appeared to Show Off Her Access
items.description: A body-worn camera recording shows Dianna Russini, formerly an “insider” for The Athletic, talking football with an officer and avoiding a citation.

items.title: Trump Pulled in at Least $2.2 Billion in 2025, Financial Disclosure Shows
items.description: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.

items.title: Neon Buys ‘Artificial,’ a Film About OpenAI, After Amazon Dropped It
items.description: Neon purchased “Artificial,” which focuses on OpenAI’s chief, Sam Altman, after Amazon walked away from it following an investment in the start-up.

items.title: Court Halts Pentagon Rule Requiring Escorts for Journalists
items.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked a new rule that restricted reporters’ access to the Pentagon, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.
